assertions.text: The Subaru was traveling northbound in the left lane at about 60 km/h.
The Subaru was northbound (direction of travel).
The Subaru was in the left lane at the time of the incident.
The front of the Subaru struck the rear of the car ahead (rear-end collision where the Subaru hit the other vehicle’s rear).
The car ahead drifted into the Subaru’s lane prior to the collision.
The driver of the Subaru braked just before the collision.
There was a single impact (the Subaru’s front hitting the other vehicle’s rear).
The other car drifted into the Subaru’s lane, then the Subaru impacted the other car’s rear.
Braking by the Subaru occurred before the collision.
The statement implies a collision occurred (no explicit severity adjective given).

verdicts.evidence: The Subaru's recorded speed was [121.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s](T2) and [111.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2), not about [60 km/h](T1); the EDR vehicle speed range is [111.0 to 121.0 km/h](T2).

The telemetry shows speed values ([121.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s](T2) and [111.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2)) but does not include an absolute heading or compass channel to confirm northbound; direction (northbound) is not reported in the provided channels (get_window_info lists speed but not heading) (T1).
Lane position (left/right lane) is not available in the telemetry channels; sensors include speed, brake, steering, and delta-V but no lane or camera/lidar data to confirm lane (T1).

The EDR labels the event as [Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)](T1) and the cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaches [ -81.0 km/h at t=0.16 s](T2),(T3) (negative indicates a forward-facing deceleration consistent with the Subaru striking something ahead).

Telemetry contains steering ([peak raw 30.0 deg at t=0.0 s](T2)) but no external-vehicle lateral position or other-vehicle tracks; drift of the other car into the Subaru's lane cannot be determined from available channels (T1,T2).

A brake application transition was detected at [t=-0.5 s](T2),(T3) (brake channel 0->1) and the last pre-impact speed decreased from [121.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s to 111.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2), consistent with braking just before impact.

The crash-pulse shows a single dominant longitudinal delta-V peak of [ -81.0 km/h at t=0.16 s](T2),(T3) and detections list one impact delta-V event for longitudinal axis (T3), consistent with a single impact.

While the sequence (other car drifts, then impact) is plausible, telemetry lacks external-vehicle lateral trajectory data or camera tracks to confirm the other car's drift; available channels do show braking at [t=-0.5 s](T2) then impact at [t=0 s / delta-V peak at 0.16 s](T3) but do not prove the preceding drift (T1,T2,T3).
Brake was applied at [t=-0.5 s](T2),(T3) and the crash impact pulse begins at [t=0 s with delta-V peaking at 0.16 s](T1),(T2), so braking occurred before the collision.
The EDR describes the event as [Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)](T1) and records a non-zero peak longitudinal delta-V of [ -81.0 km/h](T2),(T3), which indicates a collision occurred.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a high-speed frontal/rear crash with a single dominant longitudinal delta-V of [ -81.0 km/h at t=0.16 s](T2),(T3), and a brake application at [t=-0.5 s](T2),(T3). Lane and compass cannot be determined from available channels (T1,T2).

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
